programmes.show_description: (2020) Body-swap comedy-horror with Vince Vaughn. A girl gets murdered by a serial killer but then wakes up in his body, with only 24 hours to get her own body back!
Spanish Pavillion: Gordon Ramsay visits Spanish Pavillion, a family-run restaurant whose owners, brothers Michael and Jerry, spend more time struggling for power than working.
Treehouse of Horror XXX: This 666th episode of The Simpsons parodies spooky favourites like The Omen... and Crazy Rich Aliens.
Duffless: Homer fails a breathalyser test while driving home from the Duff brewery and has to quit drinking for a month.
I Love Lisa: On Valentine's Day, Lisa takes pity on Ralph and gives him a card. But when he falls in love with her, it leads to a bitter feud between their families.

programmes.show_name: Jamie: What to Eat This Week
programmes.show_description: Jamie Oliver takes us on a delicious journey as he re-awakens the spring kitchen garden after winter. He celebrates rhubarb in a lip-smacking sauce and heroes spinach in a risotto. (Ep1/3)
In London, can Phil find Sam and Krystle a house before they get hitched? Kirstie helps tall twins Matt and Chris, who are clubbing together to buy their first home.
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Neil Delamere in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.
One Hugs, the Other Doesn't: A performance by children's entertainer Nanny Gee in honour of Frederick's second birthday helps to shed some light on Frasier's romantic past.
A Diminished Rebecca with a Suspended Cliff: Rebecca hires Woody's pianist cousin to play at the bar - and he promptly falls in love with her.
License to Hill: Rebecca insists she can run the bar while Sam plays poker, but then she forgets to renew the alcohol licence.
Day 3 starts with Super-G alpine skiing finals, including reigning Paralympic champion Neil Simpson and GB's most decorated Winter Paralympian Menna Fitzpatrick in the hunt for medals.
Great Britain's match versus South Korea in the Mixed Team wheelchair curling concludes. And alpine skiing finals continue with coverage of various classifications of the Super-G events.
Channel 4's midday news summary.
Settle in for an afternoon of wheelchair curling, with GB's Jason Kean and Jo Butterfield - who has a Summer Paralympics gold from 2016 - taking on hosts Italy in the Mixed Doubles.
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Colin Jackson in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.

programmes.show_name: A New Life in the Sun
programmes.show_description: Jen and Nick set up a horse charity, Brian and Paula give French life a second shot, and coffee bar owner James brings his business idea to Spain.
The new run continues. This week's competition kicks off in Falkirk, with mum and daughter Melissa and Fiona hosting at their cosy pub. Will a fridge-less room be their downfall?
The new run continues. On night one in Manchester, Pauline plans a snug soiree with plenty of home comforts and a family feel... but she forgets to serve one of her guests! (Ep1/5)
Ade Adepitan's got all the key events from Day 3 at the Winter Paralympics, including no fewer than five British skiers bidding for medals in the Super-G skiing finals.
Channel 4's flagship news programme. Including the weather.
The Liberal Democrats: Susan Murray, MP for Mid Dunbartonshire, sets out the Liberal Democrats' campaign for a new health centre in Milngavie.
...Cooking for Less: The Sellers family mealtimes are chaos, thanks to dad Rudi's long working hours and daily supermarket trips. Will they ever get dinner on the table before 9pm?
...Standing: Horse trainer Claire believes in country air and hard work. Bambi, a city-loving model and content creator, likes the easy life. Can Bambi keep up with Claire's schedule? (Ep3)
Documentary capturing the impact of the earthquake and tsunami that devastated parts of Japan in March 2011, using amateur footage filmed by those caught up in the disaster.

programmes.show_name: Super Surgeons: A Chance at Life
programmes.show_description: Lauren, who's 30, has endured a rare cancer called myxoid liposarcoma since she was eight. Can a new surgery fix it and finally allow Lauren to get on with her life?
On the Norfolk Broads, holidaymaker Caroline needs the help of the East Anglian Air Ambulance after a boating accident. The Thames Valley team attend a large collision on the A40.
Classic American: Best pals Colleen and Naomi bought Classic American in New York together, but their inexperience is pushing their friendship to the edge. Can Gordon help?
(2024) Oscar-winning documentary offering a powerful view of destruction in the West Bank and the unlikely friendship between a Palestinian filmmaker and an Israeli journalist.
In Harrogate, Ilkley and northern Leeds, Kirstie and Phil help two young first-time buyers who are hoping to settle down, and a family who've been scarred by a previous purchase.
After months of torment, Dodger finally has his chance to expose Sully, but Sully's ready to fight dirty... Vicky bonds with Prince, Sienna makes a tough decision and Donny faces the truth.
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Colin Jackson in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.
Rich Man, Wood Man: The gang step in when Woody returns a changed man from an all-expenses-paid holiday with his rich girlfriend Kelly. Sam becomes Frasier's personal trainer.
Smotherly Love: Rebecca tries to persuade Sam to make Norm pay his bar tab after Norm has a big win on a bet. Frasier wants Lilith to confront her overbearing mother Betty.
Take Me Out of the Ball Game: Sam starts playing baseball again but struggles to fit in with his much younger teammates. Frasier loses Lilith's favourite lab rat.
It's Super-Combined finals day in the alpine skiing, representing the ultimate test of all-round skiing prowess - and GB have five athletes all in the hunt for medals.
Day 4 continues with the conclusion of GB's Mixed Team wheelchair curling clash against hosts Italy. And there's cross-country skiing action.
Channel 4's midday news summary.
The Super-Combined skiing events enter the Slalom stage, after which the medals are decided. Just the five medal hopes for GB in these - can any of them secure a spot on the podium?
The top four wheelchair curling Mixed Doubles teams meet in the semis. Billy Monger presents as they aim for the gold medal match.
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Colin Jackson in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.

programmes.show_name: A New Life in the Sun
programmes.show_description: In France, Jen and Nick must dig deep to keep their horse charity going. And will Brian and Paula's new restaurant be ready in time for opening day?
The new run continues. At Cliff and Monika's place in the countryside near Loch Lomond, one B&Ber learns that maybe too much nature can be a bad thing in their jungle-themed room.
The new run continues. In Manchester, Richard treats his guests to a rich and indulgent menu. But will undergarment unearthing and a decadent dessert be too much for some? (Ep2/5)
Menna Fitzpatrick and Neil Simpson both medalled in the Super-Combined alpine skiing for GB at the last Winter Games. Can they do it again in Italy? Ade Adepitan's got the highlights.
Channel 4's flagship news programme. Including the weather.
Plaid Cymru: Sioned Williams, Member of the Senedd for South Wales West, sets out Plaid Cymru's childcare policy.

programmes.show_name: New: Aldi vs M&S: Battle of the...
programmes.show_description: ...Brands: It's posh versus upstart. From cakes to wine Kate Quilton explores the ultimate British supermarket showdown. This isn't just any supermarket turf war, this is Aldi vs M&S. (Ep2)
...Standing: Can an eco-warrior and a petrolhead beauty salon owner agree to disagree on the environment? And a country-loving Yorkshireman shocks a London youth worker to the core. (Ep4)
What do the Goggleboxers think of Race Across the World, The Last of Us, MAFS Aus, WrestleMania, Chess Masters: The Endgame, Love on the Spectrum, and Secrets of the Penguins?
This time the Goggleboxers watch The Apprentice, Britain's Got Talent, Handcuffed: Last Pair Standing, Love Is Blind, Celebrity Puzzling, and Alexander Armstrong in India.
